note: Inherent to the JNI binding: dispatching through the per-thread JNINativeInterface vtable requires reinterpreting opaque table slots as typed function pointers. This is CWE-704 by construction, not a defect — it is the identical pattern std.DynLib.lookup uses for dlsym results, and is documented at each call site with a // SAFETY: note. The only alternative is hand-transcribing the full ~232-entry NDK vtable as a typed struct, which is more error-prone than the cast. Owner-acknowledged; kept advisory (visible, non-blocking).
See the high-severity entry: inherent JNI vtable function-pointer cast; advisory.
Inherent to the JNI binding: @alignCast pairs with the vtable/env @ptrCast (same std.DynLib.lookup pattern). Not independently fixable; advisory (visible, non-blocking).
See the high-severity entry: inherent JNI vtable alignment cast; advisory.

FFI-inherent, same class as the owner-acknowledged jni.zig entries: this file implements a C-ABI boundary (GTK/WebKit, Cocoa, WebView2/COM, JNI, or the dlopen'd plugin/launcher surface) where dispatching through foreign vtables/opaque handles requires reinterpreting untyped slots as typed pointers — CWE-704 by construction, the identical pattern std.DynLib.lookup uses for dlsym results. Not independently fixable without hand-transcribing foreign ABIs, which is more error-prone than the cast. Advisory: visible, non-blocking.

The four unwrap_or(0) are min/max window dimensions passed to gossamer_create_ex, whose FFI contract documents 0 as the 'unconstrained' sentinel (None -> no min/max). A deliberate encoding of Option across the C ABI, not an error-swallowing default.

Two expects are registration-time CString NUL checks (App::command/command_async — panicking at registration on an invalid command name is the conventional contract); the third guards the literal "{}" which cannot contain an interior NUL, so it is statically infallible.

Every unsafe block in this FFI-binding crate carries a '// SAFETY:' comment on the preceding line — exactly what this rule asks for. The matcher (regex on 'unsafe {') cannot see comments. The crate also sets #![forbid(unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn)].

Deliberate and documented at site: App::run() consumes self and gossamer_run frees the handle natively; mem::forget prevents the Drop impl from double-freeing. The second textual match is the explanatory comment.
Drop reclaims the Box::into_raw'd CommandContext pointers 1:1 (created in command()/command_async(), freed exactly once in Drop). Standard FFI user-data ownership round-trip.
CString::as_ptr passed to synchronous FFI calls while the owning CString is live in the enclosing scope; no pointer escapes the call. Standard c-string argument passing.

Reference is to the Android subproject layout under android/gossamer-android-services/ (src/main/ and src/androidTest/ exist there). SD022's existence check is root-src-relative and cannot see subproject src/ trees.

GS007 delete_remote_branches flags accumulated stale remote branches (merged feature/fix branches awaiting cleanup). This is ambient repository hygiene, not a defect in any given change, and its finding count fluctuates per-PR as branches come and go — so it must never hard-block an unrelated PR. Kept advisory (visible, non-blocking); estate policy demotes the whole git_state rule_module to advisory-by-default in the standards governance stack.
